headline: White House AI Crackdown Opens Door for China
detail: Regulatory tightening on AI exports or chip sales to China directly pressures semiconductor names. Your biggest sector exposure - SMH, SOXL, CRWD adjacent - all feel this first. This is not abstract: prior rounds of chip export rules caused double-digit drops in semis within days.
rec: No trade yet, but tighten mental stops on SOXL (leveraged, decays) and SMH if this story develops further today. If specifics name chipmakers or cloud AI, that is your trim signal on SOXL.

headline: Warsh Determined to Slay Inflation
detail: A hawkish Fed tone raises the risk of higher-for-longer rates, which compresses valuations on high-multiple tech names - exactly what fills your book. If Warsh signals rate cuts are off the table, growth and momentum names like CRWD sell off first.
rec: No action now, but watch QQQ reaction. Your stop at $670 is your line in the sand - no reason to act unless QQQ breaks $700 on volume.

headline: Oil Prices Set for Steep Monthly Drop
detail: Falling oil is generally a soft macro positive - lower input costs, less inflation pressure - which modestly helps tech sentiment. It is not a direct catalyst for your names but reduces one macro headwind.
rec: No action needed. This does not move your tech or chip holdings directly, and the AI/Fed headlines are far more relevant to your book today.

headline: We're Selling Shares of a Parabolic Mover
detail: A generic signal that extended momentum names are being trimmed by institutional players. With CRWD up nearly 5% and sitting at premium per the scanner, this is a soft reminder that parabolic moves attract profit-taking.
rec: No action today, but CRWD at $755 is in daily premium per the scanner. If it fades back to the $740-$744 scanner entry zone and holds, that is a better add point than chasing here.
